claim: Verification confirms the explorer and cross-checker claims are evidence-backed and sufficient for PRD readiness. YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit via SHA-256 file snapshots before and after full-tool Claude agents, converting any detected project mutations into BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that keep PRD readiness blocked.

evidence.summary: SHA-256 snapshot implementation verified: fileDigest() uses createHash('sha256'), buildBoundarySnapshot() walks all project files excluding allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Code paths exist and are cited correctly by both agents.

evidence.summary: Test coverage verified: two tests demonstrate boundary detection of tracked and untracked file mutations, both blocking PRD readiness
evidence.why: Test evidence supports the runtime behavior claim.

evidence.summary: Full-tool permissions verified: CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS includes Write/Edit/Bash; execution_policy sets project_mutation='forbidden' with allowed_write_roots=[artifact dir]
evidence.why: Confirms the safety model: full tools allowed, boundary audit provides protection.

evidence.summary: Blocker conversion verified: boundaryBlockers array maps changes to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers, finalReadiness sets prd_ready=false
evidence.why: This is the PRD readiness enforcement mechanism.

evidence.summary: Scope validation verified: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns blocker when brownfield claims lack project-scoped evidence
evidence.why: Prevents external-only evidence from supporting project facts.

assumptions: No unverified assumptions found in explorer or cross-checker results.
All factual claims are backed by project-scoped evidence with exact line citations.
risks: Explorer and cross-checker both identified operational risks: misconfigured allowed_write_roots could exclude project files from boundary checks, file system errors could cause missed mutations, time-of-check-to-time-of-use gaps if other processes modify files during execution.
Boundary audit only detects file system mutations, not external system changes (databases, APIs, etc.).
These risks are acknowledged limitations, not missing evidence.
missing: No missing data identified. All required slots have evidence-backed values.
Evidence agreement between explorer and cross-checker is 100% (no conflicts).
All 13 required evidence tasks completed (explorer, cross-checker, verifier).

result.notes: Verification complete. Explorer and cross-checker provided 24 evidence items with exact line citations from code, tests, and git artifacts. No assumptions presented as facts. No conflicts between agents. All evidence paths are usable and verified. Identified risks are operational concerns, not missing data. PRD readiness acceptance criteria satisfied: harness boundary audit implemented with SHA-256 snapshots, full-tool agents permitted, mutation detection converts to blockers, and scope validation prevents external-only evidence.
